message:
fix(reactor/windows): duplicate socket guard, best-effort deregister, stale handle helper

- Add duplicate socket registration check in register(): iterate existing
  registrations to detect when the same raw_socket is already registered under
  a different token, returning AlreadyExists. This mirrors the epoll reactor's
  fd reverse-index check and the kqueue reactor's equivalent guard, preventing
  ambiguous event routing when the same socket is registered twice

- Make deregister() best-effort for already-gone sockets: look up the
  registration before removing it, attempt poller.delete(), and only remove
  bookkeeping on success or when the error indicates the socket is already
  gone. Previously, the registration was removed unconditionally before the
  poller call, so a failed delete would leave the IOCP poller with a stale
  association while bookkeeping believed it was clean

- Add is_already_gone_error() helper that recognizes ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE (6),
  ERROR_NOT_FOUND (1168), and WSAENOTSOCK (10038) as indicators that the socket
  was closed or removed before deregistration — these should be treated as
  successful cleanup rather than errors

- Add test verifying duplicate socket registration returns AlreadyExists

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
